items.caption: ON THE SEA BOTTOM AND ON THE SURFACE THE DUCKS WORK LIKE MAD! RAM THOSE BALLS DOWN THE HOSE, BOYS! RAM 'EM DOWN THE HOSE!
items.md: ![ON THE SEA BOTTOM AND ON THE SURFACE THE DUCKS WORK LIKE MAD! RAM THOSE BALLS DOWN THE HOSE, BOYS! RAM 'EM DOWN THE HOSE!](CBatAotCB-175_page_1_image_1_v2.jpg)

items.caption: WE ARE, UNCA' DONALD! BUT WE'RE GETTING TIRED! THIS IS TWO SCOW LOADS WE'VE SENT DOWN!
items.md: ![WE ARE, UNCA' DONALD! BUT WE'RE GETTING TIRED! THIS IS TWO SCOW LOADS WE'VE SENT DOWN!](CBatAotCB-175_page_1_image_3_v2.jpg)

items.caption: SOON THE TERRIFIC TOIL PAYS OFF! SHE'S MOVING! HER KEEL IS PULLING OUT OF THE MUD!

items.md: DONALD DUCK - 10 - Donald and the nephews hunt gemstones in the desert, trying to match Gladstone's luck at finding jewels. (Jan. 10, 1952)

items.md: Barks has said that this story "was inspired by the desert which is just over a range of hills from San Jacinto. I've sweated through heat waves and wind storms generated by that desert so many years it is no longer fun." (Oct. 14, 1969, letter to Winston Ljungdahl)
